scope: Major headings from the big test plan plus explicit launch-confidence dimensions that are not yet first-class testing-plan sections; not every checklist bullet.

items.title: Automated feedback loops
items.notes: Phase 2 wraps the documented lint/build/test commands as verifier leaves.

items.title: Smart contracts
items.notes: Phase 5/7 should add security-review attestation and/or finer-grained contract coverage checks.
items.nextAction: Keep Hardhat suites green and use review.security.contracts to triage whether finer-grained verifier leaves are needed before release-candidate validation.
items.statusDetail: Hardhat tests run in the automated suites, but verifier does not yet attest each listed contract edge case separately.

items.title: SDK / data aggregation
items.sourceSection: 1.2 Component coverage checklist / SDK / data aggregation
items.notes: Keep filling lower-level SDK tests; later split high-value SDK invariants into separate verifier leaves if useful.
items.nextAction: Identify any SDK trust/alignment/large-dataset invariant worth promoting from ordinary tests into a dedicated verifier check.
items.statusDetail: SDK tests run in automated suites; trust/alignment/large-dataset edge-case coverage is not yet mapped to dedicated verifier checks.

items.title: Indexer / chain integration
items.sourceSection: 1.2 Component coverage checklist / Indexer / chain integration
items.notes: The dedicated indexer-integrity canary currently wraps SDK fold tests for replay/resume/idempotent duplicate batches; later indexer canaries should cover reset/reorg and live chain/indexer behavior more directly.
items.nextAction: Add reset/reorg and live chain/indexer canaries, then reference them from this verifier inventory.
items.statusDetail: Full suite includes integration coverage, and automated.indexer-integrity-canaries now tracks replay/resume/idempotent duplicate fold behavior. Reset/reorg and live Ponder replay coverage remain known gaps.

items.title: UI unit/integration tests
items.sourceSection: 1.2 Component coverage checklist / UI unit/integration tests
items.notes: UI Vitest coverage is part of test:fast and test; detailed inventory remains in ui/test-plan.md.

items.title: UI e2e tests
items.sourceSection: 1.2 Component coverage checklist / UI e2e tests
items.notes: Playwright e2e coverage is part of the full suite. IPFS/domain artifact smoke is also available as a separate release-candidate check.

items.title: AI services / generated data
items.sourceSection: 1.2 Component coverage checklist / AI services / generated data
items.notes: Avoid live model calls in ordinary verifier runs; prefer fixture/golden checks.
items.nextAction: Build a fixture/golden AI-service harness that avoids live model calls in routine verifier runs.
items.statusDetail: Seed implication regression is wrapped, but broader adversarial/golden corpora for AI services are not yet represented.

items.title: Operations / degradation automation
items.sourceSection: 1.2 Component coverage checklist / Operations / degradation automation
items.notes: The cheap operations.degradation-canary check covers representative UI dependency-failure behavior; broader stack checks are manual/guarded because they can be slow, restart services, wipe local data, or probe real staging endpoints.
items.nextAction: Extend the canary set only where the current representative slices miss a high-value failure mode, especially indexer/RPC/wrong-chain behavior.
items.statusDetail: Release-candidate validation now includes a cheap UI degradation canary for IPFS metadata, platform API, and personalization-service failures, plus guarded local-stack/IPFS/testnet smokes. Dedicated indexer/RPC/wrong-chain fault-injection canaries are still known gaps.

items.title: Performance acceptability
items.sourceSection: Verifier README / Big overarching goal / performance is acceptable
items.notes: operations.performance-budget now enforces a deterministic UI bundle-size budget as a cheap performance-regression proxy; do not infer acceptable end-to-end latency/throughput from bundle size alone.
items.nextAction: Define representative release-candidate latency/throughput thresholds and add deterministic seeded local/testnet benchmarks for key UI/API/SDK workflows.
items.statusDetail: The verifier can catch gross UI bundle-size regressions, but it does not yet measure whether key UI/API/SDK workflows are fast enough to launch under realistic seeded local/testnet conditions.

items.title: Environment checklist
items.notes: Destructive/restarting/testnet checks require explicit environment-variable opt-ins before doing work; coverage.guarded-check-policy records which guarded checks are mandatory by release-candidate and their 7-day freshness budget.
items.nextAction: Keep guarded local-stack/testnet checks current and decide which staging/mainnet smoke checks become mandatory before first deployment.
items.statusDetail: Unit/in-memory and full-suite environments are covered; Phase 6 adds guarded manual checks for fresh local stack, restarted stack, IPFS artifacts, and configured testnet staging.

items.title: Manual / LLM-driven validation roster
items.status: Phase 5 attestation checks verify that selected manual/LLM reports exist, are fresh, and use the required template; they do not run the reviews themselves.
items.notes: Do not mark subjective reviews automated merely because their reports are attested.

items.title: Cross-cutting risks checklist
items.status: Risk ownership is expected to be summarized by security and QA synthesis reports; broader per-risk coverage is still manual judgment.
items.notes: Later coverage checks should make per-risk ownership/freshness more explicit.

items.title: Known automated-test gaps
items.sourceSection: 5. Known automated-test gaps to keep filling
items.notes: Future phases should graduate the highest-value gaps into automated or attestation checks.
items.nextAction: Promote the highest-value remaining manual-plan automation backlog items into verifier checks or explicit coverage records.
items.statusDetail: This section intentionally tracks gaps. The verifier inventory records them without pretending they are covered.
